nodes.description: Browser-side gotchas — CSP surprises, Plotly constraints, WebGL context limits, and popover event traps discovered while building the UI.
headings: Gotchas — Frontend
CSP silently drops `style=""` attributes
CSSOM is exempt from the CSP — including from its *protection*
A partial preset lies quietly
markdown-it silently refuses most `data:` image URIs
`html: false` escapes raw HTML, so "not rendering" it means showing it
Shiki cannot be used for syntax highlighting, and it is not about size
Math must be MathJax SVG output, never KaTeX or MathJax-CHTML
Plotly injects a `<style>` element unless you claim its id
Plotly's automargin takes the MAX of its pushers, never the sum
`tickmode: 'array'` is the ONLY way to set ticktext, and it silently turns Plotly's tick thinning OFF
Changing a default in the template SILENTLY RELOCATES every `options` patch written against the old one
Plotly cannot read CSS variables
A linked SVG cannot be themed, so the brand mark is inlined
Retheme with `Plotly.react`, never purge + newPlot
A chart can vanish from a canvas with no error anywhere
`splom` with two dimensions draws nothing
Fills default to opaque
Plotly has no network or streamgraph trace
The `options` escape hatch is a Plotly figure fragment, merged by index
Never write a literal NUL into a source file
Re-rendering on click detaches the element that was clicked
The click that OPENS a popover will also close it, unless the anchor counts as "inside"
Highlighting search matches by string-building is two bugs, not one
A `> *` rule to make z-index work OVERRODE `position: absolute` on the cover's furniture
`api()` returns `{status, json}`, and reading the body off the wrapper fails SILENTLY
Body scroll lock does nothing here
A hover-revealed control does not exist on a touch screen
Native widget chrome ignores your dark theme
themeRiver needs real dates
swiftshader blanks gl3d in printed output while the screen looks fine
In the deck, `.md > :first-child` zeroes the margins of EVERY element
The packer measures what exists — a wrapper mounted later silently grows the sheet
A clipped table loses COLUMNS, and `max-width` will not save it
Rounded table corners need `border-collapse: separate`, and the paper override must stay `overflow: visible`
`overflow:auto` is a scrollbar on screen and a guillotine in print
A `position: fixed` toast repeats on EVERY printed page
A sheet 3 px too tall prints a silent sliver page
Constraint API quirks around custom widgets
A CSS `url()` cannot carry the per-request token, so a tokened asset gate 403s `@font-face` SILENTLY
A responsive `@media` block must sit LAST, or a same-specificity base rule beats it
Printing a presentation, every slide came out on TWO pages — and the culprit was 12px of screen gutter
The `hidden` attribute does NOT hide a `.tbtn` — an author `display` rule outranks it
Hiding an element stops the work the BROWSER owns, never the work you kicked off yourself
A document-level Esc/arrow handler must yield to EVERY open sub-surface
A document-level `paste` handler must YIELD before it PREVENTS — it sits under every input in the app
Two guards that both protect one surface are each individually unfalsifiable
`captureScreenshot` with `captureBeyondViewport` misses a below-the-fold deck sheet — the deck scrolls inside `.main`, not the page
A detached media element keeps playing
A pane that waits for three misses is OUTRUN by a fast restart
Chrome plays from a 200-only server, so a playback test cannot prove Range works
The ⌘ in a keyboard hint is the wrong glyph off macOS
A pixel offset that dodges a sibling panel breaks when the panel stops being a sibling
A drop zone sized by its CONTENT is not the drop zone the reader sees

nodes.description: Packaging constraints — what ships where (npm tarball vs HappySkills bundle), the size caps that forced the CLI migration, npx tarball testing, description validators, and scaffolding rules.
headings: Gotchas — Packaging (npm CLI & HappySkills skill)
Everything inside the skill folder ships — keep everything else out
The npm tarball is an allowlist — verify with `npm pack --dry-run`
npm FORCE-INCLUDES anything named `README*`, and a negation cannot take it back
The size caps that blocked skill publishing are solved by relocation, not shrinking
Testing a packed tarball with npx needs `-p`, or npx executes the file
A 404 on `npm view` does not mean the name is publishable
The vendored Plotly build is not interchangeable with a published dist
Description validators are strict and double-layered
Never scaffold a skill by hand
The skill loads from a mirror path
Stamping the skill CHANGELOG means RENAMING `## [Unreleased]`, not adding a section under it
`npx` with a bare spec runs the LOCAL project inside this repo — and pins everyone else to its cache
DO NOT hand-write the skill bundle's CHANGELOG — the publish step owns it

nodes.description: Kernel and CLI gotchas — process lifecycle, sockets, stdout, and state-dir traps learned while building the runtime.
headings: Gotchas — Runtime (kernel & CLI)
`process.exit` truncates large stdout
A single failed session poll is not a dead kernel
Same-version code changes do not restart a running kernel
Liveness must be health-ping, never PID
macOS `/tmp` is a symlink
Background `open` outliving its shell
Adding one field by re-serializing rewrites the whole file
A NESTED property cannot be spliced with a regex
A splice preserves the file's bytes, but not its LINE ENDING
A companion turns the forgiving markdown path into the strict one, and that would break every README
Setting a color must not change what a canvas IS
Three forgiving layers composed into a feature that did not exist — and the fix was to delete the file
⚠️ `skills-config set` REORDERS KEYS
A palette Save spawns a subprocess, and it is not instant
A validator error is a wall of red in someone's browser
A rejected file leaks its own first bytes through `JSON.parse`
Deleting collections is not `rm -rf`
A route that serves the user's files by extension must refuse a symlink
The reader's selection is state, not a repo dotfile — and it records, it never acts
Refusing a request mid-stream: answer with `Connection: close`, never `req.destroy()`

nodes.description: node:test runner traps on Node 24, plus the browser-driving traps that make the render smoke test trustworthy.
headings: Gotchas — Testing
A green suite does not mean the charts drew
`--dump-dom --virtual-time-budget` hides concurrency bugs
Never set a `Host` header on Chrome's `/json/list`
Waiting for an element does not mean the app is listening
A throwing `waitFor` in a `before` hook reports the wrong failure
A new test that cannot fail is worse than no test
An assertion that something did NOT happen is vacuous unless it COULD have happened
A test that asserts the CURRENT rendering PINS it — including the bug in it
Never poll `readAlive` in a before hook — it DELETES the kernel it fails to ping
A fixture that never contains the hard case makes the bug unfailable
Assert what the browser computed, never what the stylesheet says
A BACKTICK in a comment inside an `evaluate()` block silently detonates the whole test file
A new required field makes every negative fixture pass for the wrong reason
Subtests cannot reach parent-context servers (Node 24.0.x)
A server inside the test-runner process is invisible to subprocesses (Node 24.0.x)
An outer npm lifecycle leaks `npm_config_*` into nested npm calls
Faking `process.platform` splits workspace identity
`node --test <dir>` does not expand the directory
Set the state dir with `||=`, before requiring the registry
A security scan can trip over its own test file
Fake registry entries in shared state dirs look like leaks
A green suite proves the schemas agree; nobody was testing the prose
A synchronous `execFileSync` in a before hook FREEZES every other file's async test — and hangs the whole single-process suite
Audio never autoplays in headless Chrome without a flag, and `opts.args` REPLACES the defaults
A shim that records argv with `printf '%s\t' "$@"` invents an argument that was never passed
A piped `npm test` reports the PIPE's exit code, not the suite's
A timing-fragile browser assertion breaks when the suite gets HEAVIER, not just when the code does
A hash-only `Page.navigate` is a same-document navigation, not a reload
Headless Chrome blocks clipboard *read*, so spy on `writeText` to test a copy feature
Registering a value as a secret pollutes the whole single-process suite's redaction
An error the code under test CAUSES ON PURPOSE is not a test failure
